query: We're kicking off a new Auto-FDE engagement with the Meridian claims-ops team. Their champion Dana is on the call in an hour — run the kickoff interview with her so we end up with an engagement brief and a complete materials inventory.

here's the sharepoint export from the underwriting team — it's at ~/Downloads/uw-export/, about 300 files, mostly docx playbooks and xlsx rating templates. work through all of it and capture what's actually in there so we can plan their plugin later

i've got three SME call transcripts sitting in discovery/transcripts/ (kim-intake-call.vtt, ops-weekly-2026-06-12.txt, billing-lead.txt). pull the workflows people describe in them into digests and update the inventory status for each one

before we draft the skill catalog there are still 14 open items in .build/open-questions.json — set up a live interview with their scheduling SME and work through them so nothing in the catalog ends up being guesswork
our team lead just dropped teamdocs.zip on me — onboarding playbooks, email templates, a couple of process one-pagers. go through everything and write down how this team actually works

Start discovery for the Halvorsen finance engagement. There's no engagement.md yet, so run the kickoff with their champion first, then get to the Drive export they shared at ./materials/drive-export/.

we recorded a 90 minute walkthrough with the senior estimator yesterday, transcript at ~/meetings/estimating-walkthrough.txt. get everything she said about the bid process captured properly before it gets lost — she mentioned at least four rules that aren't written down anywhere
the digest for claims triage has a pile of entries under Open questions. i own that process — interview me, let's close them out one by one and mark them resolved

champion confirmed access to the confluence export this morning, it's synced at ./materials/confluence/. extract it into digests and then tell me what's digested, what's pending, and what's still blocked on access
kickoff's done and engagement.md is written for the fielder-ops engagement. now work the materials inventory — start with everything marked corpus
the digests are done for the metro-ops engagement — synthesize them into a skill catalog and put together the plan review page for the champion to approve
catalog's approved by the champion. scaffold the team plugin and start building the pilot skills from the discovery digests
the champion exported her notes from the review page — review-notes.md. apply her feedback on the built skills; she wants intake-triage and intake-routing merged into one
run the trigger benchmark on the finished plugin and tell me which skill descriptions are misfiring, then iterate the descriptions until we hit the accuracy target
evals passed on the underwriting plugin — package it into an installable marketplace and stand up the install guide and tutorials wiki for their team
what actually makes a good skill description? walk me through the authoring doctrine before i write the intake skill's frontmatter myself
help me prep interview questions for the data-engineer candidate we're screening thursday — i want to probe their ETL and orchestration experience without making it feel like an interrogation
extract the tables from q2-quarterly-report.pdf into a spreadsheet so finance can reconcile the numbers before close
the plugin's been live with the ops team for a month now — gather their feedback and usage pain points and improve the weakest skills based on what they hit
summarize ops-weekly-2026-06-12.txt into action items with owners for tomorrow's standup
